name: Memphis Style Method for Ribs - Weber
recipeIngredient: Salt (1 tsp per lb of meat)
Black pepper (1/4 tsp per lb of meat)
Garlic powder (1/2 tsp per lb of meat)
Paprika (1 tsp per lb of meat)
Brown sugar (1 tbsp per lb of meat)
Apple cider vinegar (1/4 cup per 3 lbs of meat)
Yellow mustard (1/4 cup per 3 lbs of meat)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the dry marinade by mixing salt, black pepper, garlic powder, paprika, and brown sugar with the amount specified for each seasoning.
2. Rub the dry marinade generously over the pork ribs, ensuring even coverage on both sides.
3. Place the ribs on a horizontal rack in a Weber kettle or grill set to low indirect heat (225°F).
4. Baste the ribs every half hour with the basting sauce mixture (equal parts apple cider vinegar and yellow mustard) until they reach an internal temperature of 165°F, which may take up to 5-9 hours.
5. About an hour before serving, stop using the basting sauce.
6. Warm the Memphis sweet BBQ sauce by heating it in a small saucepan or microwave.
7. Either brush the warm sauce over the ribs during the last 20 minutes of cooking or serve it on the side for diners to enjoy with their ribs.
8. Serve the Memphis-style smoked pork ribs hot, accompanied by the desired BBQ sauce.
